_defaults.dressCode: Modest traditional dress recommended. Follow temple-specific rules at the entrance.
_defaults.photography: Usually restricted inside sanctum areas. Check local signage before taking photos.

temples.name: Kamakhya Temple
temples.description: One of India's most revered Shakti shrines, located on Nilachal Hill in Guwahati. It is the principal temple of the Kamakhya temple complex.

temples.name: Umananda Temple
temples.location: Umananda Island, Brahmaputra River, Guwahati, Assam
temples.description: A Shiva temple on Umananda Island, also called Peacock Island, in the Brahmaputra River at Guwahati.

temples.name: Sivasagar Sivadol
temples.description: The Shiva Doul temple stands beside Sivasagar Tank with the Vishnu Doul and Devi Doul. The district administration describes it as the tallest temple of Assam.

temples.name: Navagraha Temple
temples.description: A temple of the nine planets on Chitrasal Hills, described by Assam Tourism and Kamrup Metropolitan district as an important astrological and astronomical centre.

temples.name: Hayagriva Madhava Temple
temples.description: A Vishnu temple at Hajo, noted by Assam Tourism for its blend of Hindu and Buddhist architectural styles.

temples.name: Dirgheswari Temple
temples.description: A Devi shrine in North Guwahati, described by Assam Tourism as one of Assam's important Devi Tirthas.

temples.name: Doul Govinda Temple
temples.description: A Krishna temple at the foothills of Chandrabharati Hill in Rajaduar, North Guwahati, listed by Assam Tourism.

temples.name: Ugratara Temple
temples.description: A Shakti shrine in Guwahati dedicated to Goddess Ugratara, listed by Assam Tourism among the state's temple destinations.
